hypothesis: If a large language model is continually pre-trained on domain-specific chemical and MOF-related literature before supervised fine-tuning on MOF property datasets, then it will predict MOF properties (such as band gap and gas adsorption) with lower mean absolute error than models lacking such domain-specific pre-training and competitive with or superior to graph-based and descriptor-based baselines, because exposure to specialized chemical knowledge during pre-training enables the model to learn meaningful structure-property relationships from text-based MOF representations even in low-resource fine-tuning scenarios.
